stat_of_week: 7 - Junior Caminero's 7 home runs and 15 RBI in a single week is a historically dominant individual performance, nearly single-handedly carrying Buttville Skidmarks to a 9–1 demolition of General Hospital
thriller_teams: Albuquerque Bombers vs Athletic Park Dragons
thriller_note: The Dragons actually matched the Bombers in runs scored (27–27), crushed more home runs (7–4), drove in more (34–21), and stole more bags (7–4), but Albuquerque's pitching was the difference — a 2.48 ERA and 1.07 WHIP versus a catastrophic 6.17 ERA and zero quality starts for the Dragons — and with the Dragons' luck index already inflated at +2.38, their inability to win the pitching battle in close matchups is becoming a structural problem.
key_storyline: Junior Caminero's historic 7 HR, 15 RBI, 8 runs, and .500 OBP single-handedly turned a potential competitive matchup into a 9–1 blowout, delivering the most dominant individual offensive week of the season and raising serious questions about whether Buttville Skidmarks — previously seen as a mid-tier team — are now a genuine playoff threat. Meanwhile the Bombers survived a dramatic scare from the Dragons despite being badly outclassed in hitting categories, with their pitching staff's excellence in ERA, WHIP, and QS proving once again that Albuquerque's championship ceiling is built on the most complete roster construction in the league. The Hiroshima–BaseballPro77 tie is the other major story, as both teams failed to pull away in a matchup billed as the game of the week, leaving both squads with unresolved questions heading into the playoff stretch.
lucky_reason: Despite posting a catastrophic 6.17 ERA, zero quality starts, and a 1.44 WHIP from their pitching staff, the Dragons still nearly stole a win from the Bombers on the strength of their offense — and their season luck delta of +2.38 remains the second-most inflated in the league, suggesting their record flatters their true roster quality.
unlucky_reason: The Wombats have the largest negative luck delta in the league at –2.23, and this week was no exception — Yandy Díaz posted a .536 OBP, Kumar Rocker struck out 14 with a 1.64 ERA, and the team posted a 2.63 ERA overall, yet they still lost 6–3 to the Benchwarmers, a result that continues to obscure how much genuine talent is on this roster.
power_rankings: 1. Albuquerque Bombers | Survived a 5–4 scare against the Dragons despite being outgunned offensively — the pitching staff's 2.48 ERA, 1.07 WHIP, and 3 QS won the key categories, and with a luck delta of just +0.15, this team's record is earned; the most complete roster in the league entering the playoff push | same

2. Buttville Skidmarks | Junior Caminero's 7 HR and 15 RBI in a single week is arguably the most dominant individual offensive performance of the season, and a 9–1 demolition of General Hospital vaults this team into legitimate title contention; if Parker Messick and Kyle Bradish stabilize the rotation, this team is dangerous | up

3. BaseballPro77 | A 5–5 tie with Hiroshima in the week's marquee matchup is a missed opportunity, but Gavin Williams' 14 K, Ranger Suárez's 1.50 ERA, and Jeremy Peña's 3 SB show a well-rounded roster; a luck delta of +1.27 means the record is slightly padded, but the talent is real | same

4. Hiroshima Radiation | Bryce Harper (2 HR, 7 RBI), Curtis Mead (3 HR, 5 RBI), and Drew Rasmussen's 1.50 ERA were excellent, but tying BaseballPro77 when you had 82 K and 6 QS from your pitching staff stings; a luck delta of –0.77 suggests this team is slightly underperforming its potential | down

5. Riverside Roughnecks | Hunter Goodman (4 HR, 8 RBI) and Ryan O'Hearn (2 HR, 9 RBI) gave this offense a genuine power-and-production punch in a 6–4 win over North Correa, and Jacob Misiorowski's 1.50 ERA is promising from the backend; the luck delta of +2.69 — highest in the league — remains the biggest red flag on an otherwise dangerous roster | same

6. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Foster Griffin (18 K, 0.63 ERA) and Jesús Luzardo (19 K, 1.54 ERA) were a historically dominant pitching duo in a 6–3 win over the Skeeters, and with 82.1 IP and 8 QS this team led the league in pitching volume; the offense needs to produce more consistently to make this a true contender | up

7. North Correa | Miguel Vargas (3 HR, 8 RBI), Mookie Betts (3 HR, 8 RBI), and Shohei Ohtani (6 runs, .345 OBP) gave this team an elite offensive week, but a 6–4 loss to the Roughnecks despite those numbers reveals persistent pitching fragility; the luck delta is nearly neutral at +0.23, meaning this record is an honest reflection | same

8. Sugar Land Skeeters | Michael Wacha (12 K, 1.23 ERA) and Chase Burns (10 K) anchored a solid pitching week, but the offense's 36 hits in 197 ABs and a .275 OBP in a 6–3 loss to the Twins shows the lineup continues to underperform; a luck delta of –1.00 means this team deserves better than its record suggests | down

9. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Kazuma Okamoto (3 HR, 8 RBI) and Trea Turner's speed were genuine contributors in a 7–2 win over the Wonderdogs, but zero quality starts and a 5.61 ERA from the pitching staff is deeply concerning; this team wins batting categories and hopes the opponent's pitching collapses, which is not a sustainable playoff strategy | same

10. Athletic Park Dragons | Dansby Swanson's 3 HR and 15 RBI were monster numbers and José Caballero's 3 SB gave the team speed, but a 6.17 ERA and zero quality starts in a 5–4 loss to the Bombers confirms that pitching remains an existential problem; the luck delta of +2.38 means the standings are actively lying about where this team stands | down

11. Benchwarmers | Sonny Gray's 20 K, 0.63 ERA, and 0.77 WHIP was one of the most dominant individual pitching performances of the season, and the 6–3 win over the Wombats is a quality result, but 2 HR and a .286 OBP from the offense remain chronic concerns heading into the playoff stretch | up

12. General Hospital | Andy Pages' 7 runs and Manny Machado's 2 HR were bright spots, but a 9–1 obliteration at the hands of Buttville — with only 1 quality start and 43 IP from the rotation — exposes a team that simply cannot compete in pitching categories; a luck delta of –1.15 suggests they deserve a slightly better record, but the talent gap is real | same

13. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | William Contreras (2 HR, .455 OBP) and Brooks Lee (2 HR) had solid weeks, and the team's 0.98 WHIP is legitimately elite, but only 34.2 IP and a 7–2 loss to the Reapers despite a better ERA tells the story of a team that cannot generate enough pitching volume to win consistently; a luck delta of –2.00 makes this the most snakebitten roster in the league | same

14. Canberra Wombats | Yandy Díaz's .536 OBP, Kumar Rocker's 14 K and 1.64 ERA, and a team 2.63 ERA all pointed to a competitive week, but only 48 IP and 4 QS from the staff limited their ability to win pitching volume categories in a 6–3 loss; with a luck delta of –2.23 — the worst in the league — this team's true talent is being systematically obscured by their scheduling misfortune | same
